root_motivation: Establish a single shared wire-contract for handle-based dataset results so every dh-* fleet member compiles against one set of types — preventing the drift that would otherwise arise if each crate invented its own shape.
user_persona: A Rust library consumer (another crate in the dh-* fleet, or an MCP integration test) that imports dh-spec to construct, serialize, or validate DatasetHandle / DatasetSummary / OpRequest / OpResult / Lineage values over JSON.

unfakeable_metric.name: tests_pass_count
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --workspace --release 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result' | awk '{sum += $4} END {print sum}'

acceptance_criteria.description: All public types (DatasetHandle, ColumnSchema, DatasetSummary, Capability, OpRequest, OpResult, Lineage) serialize/deserialize round-trip via serde_json with stable field names. A test asserts byte-stable JSON for a fixed fixture.
DatasetSummary never contains the full dataset: a test constructs a 10_000-row logical dataset and asserts sample.len() <= sample_cap and total serialized size < 64 KiB.
Capability enumerates exactly the nine ops named in the PRD (Aggregate, Filter, Sort, TopN, Pivot, Compare, Drill, Describe, Export). A test asserts the set.
OpResult.handle != OpRequest.handle is representable and a doc-test shows the derive-new-handle pattern.
Lineage.parents can express a multi-parent op (e.g. compare): a test builds a 2-parent lineage record.
schema/dh-spec.schema.json exists and a test validates a fixture DatasetSummary + OpResult against it.
cargo test --release passes; cargo clippy --release -- -D warnings clean.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/ac7_cargo_clean.rs — verified by cargo test --release and cargo clippy -- -D warnings exit 0

scope: Rust library crate exposing DatasetHandle, ColumnSchema, DatasetSummary, Capability, OpRequest, OpResult, Lineage types
JSON serialization/deserialization of all types via serde + serde_json
DatasetSummary::new constructor that enforces the sample cap invariant
ALL_CAPABILITIES constant array for iteration and assertion
non_goals: No execution logic — this crate only defines types and their JSON shape
No network, no async, no I/O beyond schema file generation
No operation implementations — OpRequest/OpResult are wire shapes only
No persistence, caching, or TTL enforcement — those belong to the server
No WebAssembly or embedded targets — std is required

five_whys_trace.q: Why define a separate dh-spec crate instead of putting these types inline in mqo-mcp-server?
five_whys_trace.a: Because mqo-mcp-server returns EngineResult { rows: Vec<Value> } today; every downstream crate would need to import from mqo-mcp-server just to hold a handle reference, creating an inverted dependency.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the inverted dependency matter — can't they all just agree on a shape informally?
five_whys_trace.a: The fleet has at least 14 crates (mqo-*, dh-*); informal shapes drift. mqo-spec playing this same foundation role for the MQO fleet is the direct precedent — without it crates would have drifted on measure/dimension/calc-group semantics.

five_whys_trace.q: Why is drift a problem for the dh-* fleet specifically? The types are just structs.
five_whys_trace.a: OpResult, Lineage, and DatasetSummary travel over JSON across MCP tool boundaries. A field rename or enum variant rename breaks deserialization silently — no compile error, just a wrong answer at runtime.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not just use a JSON Schema file without a Rust crate — any language can validate against that?
five_whys_trace.a: The primary consumers are Rust crates that need compile-time type safety; a JSON Schema alone does not give Rust callers typed constructors or serialization. The schema is a secondary artifact emitted from schemars so non-Rust clients (Python, TypeScript) can also validate.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should INLINE_THRESHOLD be exactly 8 or 'at most 8'?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: PRD says 'scalar or <= 8 rows'; the constant is set to 8 and the test asserts INLINE_THRESHOLD <= 8 to allow tightening.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Is the JSON Schema hand-written or generated?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Generated via schemars; the schema file is checked into schema/dh-spec.schema.json as a committed artifact.

ambiguities_resolved.question: What is the KB cap for AC2's serialized-size test?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: 64 KiB — generous enough for DEFAULT_SAMPLE_CAP=20 rows of realistic data, tight enough to catch an accidental full-dataset embed.
